text.title: AI Marketing Tool Evaluation Rules
text.summary: Four selection criteria for AI marketing tools — integration fit, automation capability, management overhead, ROI tracking — and the anti-patterns that cause tool overload and stack fragmentation.

rule.statement: Evaluate each tool against four criteria before adoption: (1) integrates with existing stack without manual data export/import, (2) automates at least one currently manual handoff, (3) management overhead is below the time saved, (4) ROI is measurable within 30 days.
rule.rationale: Tool overload is the primary AI marketing adoption failure mode. Teams adopt tools without a coherent stack, creating data silos and manual reconnection work that eliminates the productivity gain. Strict selection criteria prevent accumulation.

antipattern.description: Adopting a tool for every new marketing function without evaluating integration with the existing stack.
antipattern.reason: Creates data silos requiring manual export/import between tools. The reconnection work often exceeds the automation benefit, resulting in negative ROI.

antipattern.description: Using AI SEO tools (Surfer, Clearscope) and following their keyword density recommendations mechanically without human brand voice review.
antipattern.reason: Keyword density optimization produces content that ranks but reads awkwardly. Agents following SEO tool recommendations blindly produce keyword-stuffed content. Human must balance SEO scoring against readability and brand voice.

antipattern.description: Adopting multi-channel distribution tools before defining channel-specific content conventions.
antipattern.reason: Distribution tools amplify process. Without defined conventions for LinkedIn vs. Twitter vs. newsletter format, automated variants are too similar to each other and lose channel-native feel.

rule.statement: Track ROI per tool category: content velocity (pieces per week), content quality (rankings, CTR), ad performance (ROAS), and automation savings (hours/week recovered).
rule.rationale: 77% marketing adoption and 50%+ of gen AI enterprise budgets directed at sales/marketing means budget scrutiny is high. Tools without measurable ROI within 30 days should be deprioritized or replaced.
